content.problemStatement:
Chef has NNN points (numbered 111 through NNN) in a 2D Cartesian coordinate system. For each valid iii, the iii-th point is (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xi‚Äč,yi‚Äč). He also has a fixed integer ccc and he may perform operations of the following type: choose a point (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xi‚Äč,yi‚Äč) and move it to (xi+c,yi+c)(x_i + c, y_i + c)(xi‚Äč+c,yi‚Äč+c) or (xi‚ąíc,yi‚ąíc)(x_i - c, y_i - c)(xi‚Äč‚ąíc,yi‚Äč‚ąíc).

Now, Chef wants to set up one or more checkpoints (points in the same coordinate system) and perform zero or more operations in such a way that after they are performed, each of his (moved) NNN points is located at one of the checkpoints.

Chef's primary objective is to minimise the number of checkpoints. Among all options with this minimum number of checkpoints, he wants to choose one which minimises the number of operations he needs to perform.

Can you help Chef find the minimum number of required checkpoints and the minimum number of operations he needs to perform to move all NNN points to these checkpoints?
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚čÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚čÖ105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and ccc.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers xix_ixi‚Äč and yiy_iyi‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing two integers ‚Äē the minimum number of checkpoints and the minimum number of moves.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: One optimal solution is to set up checkpoints at coordinates (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1) and (1,0)(1, 0)(1,0). Since the points (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1) and (1,0)(1, 0)(1,0) are already located at checkpoints, Chef can just move the point (3,2)(3, 2)(3,2) to the checkpoint (1,0)(1, 0)(1,0) in two moves: (3,2)‚Üí(2,1)‚Üí(1,0)(3, 2) \rightarrow (2, 1) \rightarrow (1, 0)(3,2)‚Üí(2,1)‚Üí(1,0).
